description:
You are helping a developer who keeps a very small project under
  /home/user/my_project.
  The developer would like to catalogue the sizes of the three project files
  (main.py, utils.py, and README.md) inside a tiny SQLite database so that
  later tools can read the information programmatically.

  Your job – performed strictly from a Linux terminal – is to:

  1.  Create a new SQLite database called
      /home/user/my_project/db/project_files.db with a single table:
         file_info (filename TEXT PRIMARY KEY, size_bytes INTEGER).

  2.  Insert one row into that table for each of the three files that are
      already present in /home/user/my_project.  
      • filename must be exactly the file’s basename  
      • size_bytes must be the file’s size in bytes obtained from the
        filesystem at the moment you run the command.

  3.  Produce a *plain-text* SQL dump of the entire database (schema **and**
      data) and save it to
      /home/user/my_project/db/project_files_dump.sql.

      The test-suite will look for the following exact, newline-terminated
      seven-line structure in the dump file (case-sensitive, no extra
      whitespace).  Replace XX, YY, and ZZ with the correct integer byte
      sizes you discover for main.py, utils.py, and README.md respectively.

        PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF;
        BEGIN TRANSACTION;
        CREATE TABLE file_info (filename TEXT PRIMARY KEY, size_bytes INTEGER);
        INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('main.py',XX);
        INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('utils.py',YY);
        INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('README.md',ZZ);
        COMMIT;

      • The order of those INSERT lines must be exactly as shown above.  
      • Every line must end with a single newline character, including the
        final COMMIT; line.

  4.  Append a short two-line confirmation log to
      /home/user/my_project/db/db_log.txt *after* the database work
      completes.  The file must already exist or be created by you and must
      contain exactly these two lines (each followed by a newline) – no extra
      spaces or blank lines:

        Database project_files.db created successfully.
        Dump file project_files_dump.sql generated successfully.

  Important notes for passing automated checks:
  • Work entirely as the normal user (no sudo).  
  • Do not move, edit, or rename the original three project files.  
  • Ensure both the database and the dump are placed in
    /home/user/my_project/db/ (create the directory if it is missing).  
  • The test harness will open the dump file and compare it verbatim to the
    seven-line template above, with your discovered numbers substituted for
    XX, YY, and ZZ.  
  • The harness will also check that db_log.txt contains exactly the two
    required lines, nothing more, nothing less.

  Because only two or three shell commands are required (one to create &
  populate the DB, one to dump, and one to append the log), try to finish
  succinctly.
truth:
<!--
       Pre-existing project structure (created inside the container before the
       agent starts):

         /home/user/my_project/
           ├── main.py
           ├── utils.py
           └── README.md

       Exact file contents (including final newline) so the automated tests
       can deterministically calculate size_bytes:

       1) /home/user/my_project/main.py
          print("Hello, World!")\n
          -> 23 bytes

       2) /home/user/my_project/utils.py
          def add(a, b): return a + b\n
          -> 28 bytes

       3) /home/user/my_project/README.md
          # My Project\n
          This is a sample project structure.\n
          -> 49 bytes

       Therefore the correct INSERT lines that must appear in the dump file,
       in order, are:

         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('main.py',23);
         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('utils.py',28);
         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('README.md',49);

       The full, exact content that the grader will expect in
       /home/user/my_project/db/project_files_dump.sql is:

         PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF;
         BEGIN TRANSACTION;
         CREATE TABLE file_info (filename TEXT PRIMARY KEY, size_bytes INTEGER);
         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('main.py',23);
         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('utils.py',28);
         INSERT INTO file_info VALUES('README.md',49);
         COMMIT;

       Each line must end with a single UNIX LF byte (0x0A).

       The grader will also verify that
       /home/user/my_project/db/db_log.txt exists with *exactly*:

         Database project_files.db created successfully.\n
         Dump file project_files_dump.sql generated successfully.\n
  -->
name: task_000000_05b3fa69
